REQUEST_CHANGES
`internal/core/loop.go:316` / `internal/core/loop.go:317` set `CacheReadAmount` and `CacheWriteAmount` pointers unconditionally during configured-cost computation, even when `resp.Usage.CacheRead == 0` and `resp.Usage.CacheWrite == 0`. That makes explicit zero mean both “no reported cache tokens under configured pricing” and `CachePolicy="off"`, contradicting the required convention that explicit zero identifies op…
artifact: .ddx/executions/20260425T155315-f96c0a7e/reviewer-stream.log

post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
`internal/core/loop.go:316` / `internal/core/loop.go:317` set `CacheReadAmount` and `CacheWriteAmount` pointers unconditionally during configured-cost computation, even when `resp.Usage.CacheRead == 0` and `resp.Usage.CacheWrite == 0`. That makes explicit zero mean both “no reported cache tokens under configured pricing” and `CachePolicy="off"`, contradicting the required convention that explicit zero identifies opt-out and nil means not reported/unknown.
`go test ./... -count=1` was not verifiable in this sandbox because multiple existing tests using `httptest.NewServer` panic on localhost bind with `socket: operation not permitted`; rerun in an environment that permits local test servers.
